export_view.value:
As the year ends, many stores must provide a complete inventory valuation for accounting and tax purposes. The Inventory Valuation Report enables users to capture the total value (i.e., cost) of on-hand inventory at a specific moment.

We recommend taking periodic "snapshots" of your inventory and its valuation to maintain accurate historical records. These snapshots provide a precise point-in-time inventory value that can be referenced later. Remember to run the report on the exact date and time you want to capture.

The Inventory End-of-Year (EOY) Snapshot Archive is a valuable tool for stores needing a complete inventory valuation for accounting or tax purposes. It provides a downloadable document listing all items with an active inventory status as of December 31st, including quantity, inventory status, cost, and more. Snapshots are available on January 1st of the new year.

Keep in mind that inventory is constantly moving and changing. To accurately preserve a snapshot, users must export the data. RepairQ does not retain a history of prior inventory levels, so the counts will differ each time the report is run.

Additional Resources

  • Reports: Inventory Valuation

  • Reports: Inventory End of Year (EOY) Snapshot Archive

  • Reports: Inventory Usage

  • Reports: Inventory Shrinkage

  • Inventory Status Overview

Capture a Valuation Snapshot

On the Navigation Bar, click Reports. On the Reports page, click the Inventory dropdown along the top row of report categories. From the available list, click Valuation Summary. If you prefer to view results sorted by each inventory item, select Valuation Detail.

Use the filters to customize your Inventory Valuation Report based on specific parameters. For best results, generate separate reports for each inventory status to account for each segment individually. If you need a total valuation of all on-hand inventory, you can include all statuses in the same report.

  • Date Range - Leave this field blank. Click the Cancel (X) button to clear any existing entries.

  • Date Type - Set to "Date Created."

  • Location - Choose a specific location or run an aggregate report across multiple stores.

  • Status - Select all non-consumed inventory statuses you want to include in the valuation. Be sure to include transition statuses and any custom inventory statuses your organization tracks for on-hand inventory, as these items are still in your store. For more information on inventory statuses, check out our article: Inventory Status Overview. Our recommendation for this valuation snapshot includes the following inventory statuses:

    • Instock

    • Pulled

    • Pending Refurb

    • Pending Restock

    • Pending RMA

    • Pending Transfer

    • Overstock (Pending RMA)

    • Received for Refurb

    • Other

    • Any additional custom inventory statuses

  • Condition - Leave blank (no selection).

  • Supplier - Leave blank (no selection).

  • Include Inactive Catalog Items - Check this box if you need detailed catalog item information.

After making your selections, click Apply Filters to generate the report. To export the data, click the To Excel button, which will download an Excel-compatible document.

Important: Be sure to export the report immediately. The inventory data reflects the exact moment you run the report, and counts will change if the report is generated again later.

Valuation Summary and Detail

If you want to view figures for specific inventory statuses, you can run the report multiple times, selecting a different set of statuses for each run. For example, you might generate one report for Instock inventory, another for Pending RMA, and so on.

Valuation Summary

This report aggregates all inventory records by item type based on the selected statuses. Totals for the selected statuses appear at the bottom of the report.

Valuation Detail

This report provides itemized records for each specific inventory item and its quantities. Totals for each row are displayed in the Combined Item Cost column.

START 1.22.X
